description:
Build a voice-controlled court docket management module for a court clerk who uses Talon Voice. The pre-seeded file C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\TalonTasks\court_docket.csv contains 80 active court cases with columns: case_number, case_type, defendant, plaintiff, attorney, judge, next_hearing (ISO datetime like 2025-04-07 09:00), courtroom, status, priority.

Create the directory %APPDATA%\Talon\user\docket_manager\ containing three files:

docket_manager.py: A Talon Python module that on load reads the CSV and populates a dynamic user.docket_cases list (keys: case numbers with hyphens removed, values: actual case numbers). Implement four actions:
- user.docket_lookup(case_number: str) — show full case details via app.notify()
- user.docket_by_judge(judge_last_name: str) — filter cases by judge last name, group by courtroom, sort each group by hearing time, and write a formatted docket sheet to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\docket_<judge>_<YYYYMMDD>.txt with a header line, per-courtroom sections, and a total case count at the end
- user.docket_continue(case_number: str, new_date: str) — update that case's next_hearing to new_date, set its status to Continued, re-sort the entire CSV by next_hearing date, overwrite the CSV file, and refresh the dynamic list
- user.docket_summary() — show a notification with the count of cases per case_type

case_types.talon-list: A Talon list named user.case_types mapping 8 spoken forms to their CSV codes: felony to FL, misdemeanor to MS, civil to CV, family to FM, traffic to TR, juvenile to JV, probation violation to PV, restraining order to RO.

docket.talon: Voice commands mapping: pull case <user.docket_cases> to docket_lookup, docket for judge <user.text> to docket_by_judge, continue case <user.docket_cases> to <user.text> to docket_continue, and docket summary to docket_summary.
